name: Baganda
description: The Baganda are the largest ethnic group in Uganda and the people of the Buganda Kingdom — one of the most politically sophisticated kingdoms in pre-colonial Africa. Founded in the 14th century, Buganda reached its height in the 19th century with a standing army, a complex bureaucracy, and a Kabaka (king) whose authority was supported by a parliament (Lukiiko). The Baganda were early adopters of literacy, Christianity, and formal governance, which gave them outsized influence in colonial and post-independence Uganda.
cultural_highlights: Buganda Kingdom — one of Africa's oldest and most complex kingdoms, with over 52 clans (ebika) each with specific totems and roles
Kabaka (king) institution — the Kabaka is the symbol of Buganda unity; the current Kabaka Ronald Muwenda Mutebi II was coronated in 1993
Ssekabaka tombs (Kasubi) — the royal tombs at Kasubi Hill are a UNESCO World Heritage Site, housing the remains of four kabakas
Barkcloth (olubugo) — the traditional fabric of Buganda, made from the Mutuba tree; a UNESCO Intangible Heritage
Nnankasa dance — a courtly dance traditionally performed for the Kabaka, now a cultural showpiece

food_traditions.name: Matoke (steamed banana)
food_traditions.description: East African highland cooking banana steamed in banana leaves — the soul food of Buganda and a Ugandan national dish

food_traditions.name: Groundnut stew (Ebinyebwa)
food_traditions.description: Rich peanut sauce cooked with chicken, beef, or offal, served with matoke or sweet potato

food_traditions.name: Nsenene (grasshoppers)
food_traditions.description: Seasonal flying grasshoppers fried with onion and salt — a prized Buganda delicacy, both a snack and a celebratory dish

music_traditions: Amadinda xylophone — a large log xylophone central to royal court music, played by multiple performers simultaneously
Akadinda — a smaller xylophone with interlocking musical parts, requiring deep ensemble coordination
Baakisimba dance — the most widespread Baganda social dance, featuring hip-rolling movements and call-and-response drumming
